AC: 30
STR: +7 WIS: +7 INT: +7 HP: +100 MANA: +100 ENDUR: +100
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

AC: 30

rot loot
QuoteReply
# Oct 06 2004 at 9:32 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591814647032
9 posts
I got this tonight as rot loot. Watched a 2 group raid force drop him, 2 of these and the mask, I got the second one, since everyone in the raid had better and only one person chose to bring an alt over to get it.


Skitch
▲ Page top
3 Rings...
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2003 at 10:48 PM Rating: Decent
Seaxouri
View User Forum
112 posts
Tonight he dropped 3 rings for us, also the legs. Last time we killed him he dropped 2 rings.
▲ Page top
Medding
QuoteReply
# Aug 06 2003 at 10:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249809871
4 posts
Just so you know... if you use 1000 mana to buff, even if that mana is all your mana, or only 20% of your total mana, you still have to meditate to regain that 1000 mana, which takes the same amount of time either way. Therefore, it does not decrease downtime, just lets you fight for longer before stopping.
▲ Page top
RE: Medding
QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2004 at 1:03 AM Rating: Decent
dafdaf
View User Forum
Scholar
1,273 posts
you're absolutely right in theory, but in practice it's better than that. You eventually have to stop pulling because:
a) someone died
b) no more mobs
c) someone needs to med/buff

in each case there can be a point where you could become fom and still have to wait for whichever of the situations above to change. With a greater mana capacity, you can therefore be filling up further, and therefore last longer than you would otherwise.

After a point it does just become silly having lots of mana though, and mana regen becomes a Lot more useful i must admit.
▲ Page top
RE: Medding
QuoteReply
# Apr 17 2004 at 1:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257248882
Scholar
155 posts
Hehe kill a mob that takes 10-15 minutes to kill and say that a large mana pool is overkill. Even on a mob like this, if your clerics all have a low mana pool, you are gonna need a legion of necro's to keep those heals coming. Or two dozen clerics. Endgame especialy, large mana pool > almost everything for healers and nukers... I'm not elemental yet, but have already been in on fights where if our healers hadn't had 5k+ mana pools, it woulda been over. I have seen 5k healers go OOM in a single fight. Lot of people are spoiled by the low HP/High damage PoP bosses that are all about DPS. Don't forget, there are still some long **** fights out there, where a huge mana pool is imperative.
▲ Page top
RE: Medding
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2004 at 3:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591783489353
View User Forum
8 posts
Just make two groups of 4 clerics and two necro's each. Have a caster mob oftanked and mindwreck away. Your healers won't go oom on a rotation then.

Edited, Tue Jun 1 04:25:25 2004
▲ Page top
RE: Medding
QuoteReply
# May 13 2004 at 5:41 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592255076925
View User Forum
172 posts
you have it reversed. for extremely long fights only mana regen is extremely important, for short fights mana pool makes the bigger difference.

IE in a 30 minute fight, there are 300 ticks, FT15 + VoQ + medding + bard + SD + pot9 = about 100 mana/tick, ie 30,000 mana over 30 minutes. do you really think 4k mana pool vs a 6k mana pool makes much of a difference in this situation?
▲ Page top
=(
QuoteReply

28 posts

QuoteReply
# Sep 22 2003 at 7:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259022482
View User Forum
82 posts
Rarity Rating takes into account the rarity of the mob. Since you can't drop this guy every day, all his loot is deemed rare or more.
▲ Page top
RE:
QuoteReply
# Dec 08 2003 at 8:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765834360
Sage
111 posts
Don't think it does. I believe the rarity rating is just how common the drop is from the mob in question. That's the way it's supposed to be at least, but it doesn't mean that Alla will match all your experiences. Looking at the posts it seems very random and luck plays in I suppose, but Alla still get their information from what people report so it's depending on the publics feedback.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 12 2003 at 7:11 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is the type of item best handed to a caster of any type. I think the melee classes can use this but is better suited for any of the casters, that are nuking/buffing. Lets face it people more mana from them allows for more nukes, and shorted delays after routine buffing times.
RE: Who to
QuoteReply
# May 14 2004 at 1:49 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595780199550
View User Forum
Scholar
178 posts
30 AC on a ring makes this a tank-drool item. The stats, HP/mana and such are nice for whoever gets it, but that kind of AC on a finger item is only available from PoTime (1 VT drop, I think). I can get a better caster ring (counting foci/effects) from a number of sources all much easier to take out than AoW.


Edited, Fri May 14 14:49:08 2004
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 18 2003 at 7:39 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) oh my, not another one of these "who should get this posts"....30ac for a ring!!! i really hope no caster is taking aggro as to need 30ac, and if they do as low as their ac is anyhow it wont help.
#REDACTED, Posted: Dec 19 2002 at 8:29 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ypu that simple, this is a major shammy item. why? +7wis, +100mana, +100hp thats why. they gain over 100 mana from this and the 100 extra hp makes it so they can get more mana by cann'ing, so i personally would give this to shammy's first then hybrids but never warrior's, they suck too much.
RE: major shammy item
QuoteReply
# May 24 2003 at 1:34 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591787147116
1 post
LOL grray...
My guess is you play a shaman... Any class in the game benefits from having extra hp and mana, and it adds as much int as it does wis, making just as good for int casters. Give a ring that is equally beneficial to almost every class to shamans first? you gotta be kidding me

They didn't kill it. Heard it was at around 15 percent when they got adds. AOW difficulty is far, far away from Statue of Rallos Zek. The Statue is amazingly easy, King Tormax is easy, Avatar of War is HARD. lol it steamrolled over me that day when I logged on in Kael. Hit very, very fast for anywhere from 700-1100 per hit.

Zyrrael
Human Warlord of Torvonnilous

#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 15 2002 at 11:20 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) basically the Avatar of War HAD only been killed on Nameless by two guilds who used some good strategy, then that strategy was nerfed, so someone must have gotten the forces or found a nice strategy to kill AoW, i didn't think this would come as soon as it did =)
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 08 2002 at 12:53 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) on your profile you had a keyring. is that really an item you can get? or its just a way you can show your keys?

QuoteReply
# Apr 02 2002 at 2:02 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248159931
33 posts
It's not an item, it's an option on Magelo.com to put your 'keys' to dungeons in, so they can be organized.
